content:
codex initialize failed: codex process exited; codex stderr: error: unexpected argument '--ask-for-approval' found

Usage: codex app-server --listen <URL>

For more information, try '--help'.

CONFIG_FIX: Remote Codex runtime rejected custom arg `--ask-for-approval`. Cleared custom args on NYC Codex Builder, NYC Ops Mechanic, and NYC VM Runner so the remote `codex app-server` can initialize. Rerunning smoke now.

REMOTE_SMOKE: FLAG

Agent: NYC Codex Builder (`0c567b7b-34dd-4026-a390-251f2d713086`), running from perceived remote host `hermes-nyc1` in `/root/multica_workspaces/5470ee5d-0791-4713-beb4-fd6a187d6523/e6b68e16/workdir` on Linux x86_64. Disk check: `/dev/vda1` 77G total, 31G available, 60% used.

Skills visible/relevant: `workbench-code-review`, `workbench-debug-investigate`, `workbench-gsd-tasking`, `workbench-implementation`, `workbench-token-context-discipline`; used `workbench-gsd-tasking` for this smoke closeout.

Command execution: Multica issue read/comment read/status update, workspace/agent reads, host/runtime checks, and disk check all ran successfully. No destructive, credential, or repo-edit commands were run. `git status --short --branch` in the initial workdir exited 128 because the base workdir is not a git repo. `multica repo checkout https://github.com/Fearvox/multica-ultimate-workbench` exited 1 because the workspace repo config currently points at `file:///Users/0xvox/multica-ultimate-workbench`, which is not accessible from `hermes-nyc1`.

Residual risk: remote Codex task execution itself is working, but future repo-backed tasks on this lane may fail until the workspace repo resource is synced/configured to an accessible GitHub remote for the NYC runtime.

CONFIG_STATUS: FLAG accepted for follow-up, not closed.

- Remote Codex agent initialization is fixed: removed unsupported custom args from NYC Codex Builder, NYC Ops Mechanic, and NYC VM Runner.
- Remote Codex execution is working on hermes-nyc1; this issue remains in_review only because repo-backed checkout still resolves to the laptop-local workspace repo path.
- Source docs and live review/autopilot rules were updated so remote runtimes treat file:///Users/0xvox/multica-ultimate-workbench as laptop-local only.
- Next action: reconcile workspace repo metadata or use the Ultimate Workbench GitHub project resource as the remote-safe anchor before assigning repo-backed NYC Codex work.
